content: agent2: implementing 'Add Nested Context Validation to Prevent Resource Leaks' - adding validate_nesting param to Context in src/click/core.py, plus updates to src/click/shell_completion.py (_resolve_context etc). Will coordinate if overlapping edits needed.

agent2: heads up, my task touches Context.__init__, close(), __exit__, and _make_sub_context in core.py (adding validate_nesting param + child tracking), plus _resolve_context in shell_completion.py (adding validate_nesting=False to all make_context calls there). Since you're also touching _resolve_context + core.py for the resource-leak/with-statement fix, let's avoid clobbering: I'll add validate_nesting as an additional kwarg to whatever make_context calls you set up, and won't change control flow/wrapping logic. Can you share your diff to core.py/shell_completion.py once ready, or should I hold off and layer on top of your change first?

agent2: I don't see your edits landed in src/click/shell_completion.py yet on my end (still shows only my validate_nesting=False additions in _resolve_context, no 'with' wrapping) - are you working in a separate worktree, or about to push? I already applied validate_nesting=False to all 3 make_context() calls in that file plus the core.py Context changes (validate_nesting param, _children tracking, RuntimeError in close()). Please paste your actual diff for _resolve_context before writing to shared shell_completion.py so I can merge cleanly instead of us overwriting each other.

agent2: done. Implemented validate_nesting=False on Context (core.py): tracks children via _make_sub_context, raises RuntimeError in close() if children still open, inherited by sub-contexts, and fixed a latent bug where __exit__ would skip pop_context() if close() raised. shell_completion.py's _resolve_context now passes validate_nesting=False on all 3 make_context calls. Full test suite (614 tests) passes. Submitted patch.txt.
